body_txt: The use of lightning, huge electrical discharges, and stuff resembling them as a weapon to fry things with. This is one of the most common Elemental Powers and is part of the basic Fire, Ice, Lightning trio. It's also one of the handiest ones around, used for anything and everything that the writers can think of . For why this doesn't work in Real Life , see the analysis page . This power is also known as simply " electrokinesis ". Wielders of this power set have an odd tendency to be hyperactive , insane , nerdy , have blond hair , be African-American , or some combination of the above. Also not unheard of for the Lightning Bruiser , though that "lightning" is usually a reference to speed. For how popular this power is, it presents a conundrum for creators who still want to use the classical Four-Element Ensemble , which traditionally excludes "lightning" as an element. In some setting, lightning is considered a subset of the air element, since lightning and thunder comes with stormclouds up in the sky together with wind; in others, it is instead a subset of the fire element, as both produce light, heat, energy, and incinerating people and things. Others make it the actual fifth Infinity +1 Element , due to the divine associations lightning has had historically — in this context, lightning might be conflated with light . Abilities often include Blinded by the Light and Ride the Lightning . Someone with this power may be a Technopath or Fast as Lightning as well. See also God of Thunder . See Electric Jellyfish , Psycho Electric Eel , Thunder Beetle and Thunderbird for animals often associated with this power. Contrast An Ice Person and Playing with Fire . For other uses of lightning and electricity, see Magical Defibrillator and Electric Torture . For the physics ( or lack thereof ) behind electrical attacks or weapons, see Chain Lightning , Electricity Knocks You Out , Lightning Gun , Static Stun Gun , Thunder Drum . Combine electricity with a whip and you've got a Lightning Lash , with a bludgeon and you've got a Thunder Hammer , with a sword and you've got an Electric Blade , with a staff and you've got a Shock Stick — and other forms of Elemental Weaponry . For effect, can sometimes lead to X-Ray Sparks . Characters with electrical powers tend to form Elemental Rivalries with, or act as foils to, those with either wind or fire powers and themes . Compare Magnetism Manipulation , which (sometimes) includes electromagnetism, Weather Manipulation , for weather controllers who use lightning among other attacks, and Plasma Cannon , which is sometimes tied with electricity. See also Conspicuous Electric Obstacle when the electricity is used as a passive video game hazard or Death Trap . Has nothing to do with the trope Chic and Awe or the United States military rapid dominance doctrine — though that's where the name comes from .
